page.content:
In the context of our ongoing research into the differentiation potential of various samples, the data presented in the accompanying table offers critical insights into the crystallite size and its correlation with specific differentiation stages. Notably, the "Crystallite Size (nm)" column highlights the varying physical characteristics of the samples, which are hypothesized to influence their biological behavior. For instance, a crystallite size of 12.2 nm in sample TM0 may suggest a favorable structural environment for differentiation processes.

Furthermore, the metrics related to differentiation stages—specifically, the "Stage 1" and "Stage 2" columns—provide quantitative measures of differentiation efficacy, with corresponding p-values to evaluate statistical significance.

page.content: The observed p-value of 0.044* indicates a noteworthy difference between the stages, suggesting a statistically significant impact of the crystallite size on the differentiation outcome. Collectively, these metrics underscore the relevance of physical properties in biological applications, guiding future investigations into material modifications aimed at optimizing differentiation protocols.
